sections.paragraph:
ἀνεξιχνίαστος, ον (ἀ-, ἐξιχνιάζω ‘track out’; Cat. Cod. Astr. VIII/2 p. 156, 16; Etym. Mag. p. 709, 50; Job 5:9; 9:10; 34:24; Prayer of Manasseh [=Odes 12] 6) lit. ‘not to be tracked out’, inscrutable, incomprehensible, of God’s ways Ro 11:33. Of the riches in Christ fathomless Eph 3:8 (RThomas, ET 39, 1928, 283). ἀ. δημιουργία inscrutable creation Dg 9:5.—1 Cl 20:5. Cp. FPfister, SBHeidAk 1914, no. 11, p. 8.—DELG s.v. ἴχνος. TW. Sv.

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 77
